# Retrieval For Coding Projects

## Summary

Coding-project retrieval works best when knowledge is small, structured, source-backed, and easy to rank. The goal is not to store everything in context; the goal is to retrieve the few sections that explain the current task.

## Recommended Files

- `AGENTS.md`: how agents should work in the repo, including safety, style, source order, tests, and workflow.
- `README.md`: product purpose, setup, common commands, and high-level architecture.
- `docs/`: stable architecture, runbooks, release process, and provider-specific notes.
- `MEMORY.md`: curated durable decisions, constraints, conventions, and facts that should survive threads.
- `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`: short-term notes that are useful soon but may not be durable.
- Project Knowledge pages: reusable task guidance, route cards, source notes, and operating standards.

## Page Shape

Use this structure for pages that should retrieve well:

```md
---
title: Short Exact Title
aliases: [terms users naturally say, old feature names]
tags: [runtime, retrieval, verification]
---

# Short Exact Title

## Summary

One compact paragraph that says when the page matters.

## Rules

- Specific operating rules.
- Source boundaries and precedence.

## Sources

- Official or repo-local source links.
```

## Retrieval Rules

- Prefer exact title, alias, heading, and file-name matches before semantic search.
- Keep section headings stable; they are retrieval targets.
- Put the highest-value summary near the top of each page.
- Preserve provenance: title, relative path, heading, and source link.
- Return a small ranked packet, not a whole folder.
- Avoid duplicated pages that compete with canonical repo docs.
- Use route-card pages for task families and source-note pages for evidence.

## What To Avoid

- giant prompt manuals
- generated summaries with no source path
- stale duplicate copies of README or release docs
- pages that mix unrelated topics
- private notes, secrets, tokens, or local machine paths
- vector search as a first response to weak headings or bad page names

## Sources

- OpenAI retrieval guide: https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/retrieval
- Anthropic contextual retrieval: https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/contextual-retrieval
- Lewis et al., Retrieval-Augmented Generation: https://arxiv.org/abs/2005.11401
- Vicode Project Knowledge retrieval plan: ../../project-knowledge-retrieval-plan.md
